# AgentVitals

> The AI readiness check for hospital websites. A free diagnostic tool that evaluates how well AI agents can understand, navigate, and book appointments on hospital and health system websites.

## About

AgentVitals scores hospital websites across four pillars:

- **Semantic Richness** (25%): Structured data, Schema.org markup, JSON-LD, OpenGraph metadata
- **Actionability Surface** (30%): Booking links, appointment scheduling, digital pathways vs phone-only, CTAs
- **DOM Navigability** (25%): ARIA labels, semantic HTML, accessibility, server-rendered content
- **Integration Protocol Readiness** (20%): robots.txt, llms.txt, sitemap.xml, FHIR endpoints, OpenAPI specs, AI plugin manifests

Each pillar receives a letter grade (A through D) and the site receives an overall AI Readiness score out of 100.

## Why It Matters

AI agents are already attempting to search for doctors, check availability, and book appointments on hospital websites autonomously. Hospitals whose websites cannot be navigated and transacted with by these agents are invisible to a growing channel of patient acquisition.

## Who Built This

AgentVitals is a joint effort by [HospitalWebsites.com](https://hospitalwebsites.com) and [Sparkle by Combinaut](https://www.sparkle.health/). Sparkle CMS is purpose-built for hospital websites with a Find-a-Doctor application designed from the ground up for agentic AI — structured data, API-first provider search, bookable appointment slots, and FHIR integration.

## Links

- Web App: https://hospital-analyzer.vercel.app
